title: Idea
description: You don't have an idea yet, or you have one but don't know if it's worth building. Use AI to brainstorm, Reddit to validate, SimilarWeb to check if competitors exist. *"[Name] helps [who] do [what] so they can [benefit]."* If you can't finish that sentence clearly, the idea isn't clear enough yet.

options.startHere: **Claude** or **ChatGPT** — prompt: *"Give me 10 SaaS ideas for [your niche/skill]"*
options.alternative: Perplexity

options.need: See if people actually want it
options.startHere: **Reddit** — search your problem, see if people complain about it
options.alternative: GummySearch 🟢
Ahrefs 💳
Google Trends 🟢
Indie Hackers 🟢

title: Research & Planning
description: Turn your idea into a plan. What pages does it need? What data does it store? What does a user actually *do* on it? AI is your free product manager here.

options.startHere: **Claude** — prompt: *"Write a product requirements doc for [your idea]"*
options.alternative: ChatGPT

options.startHere: **Claude** — prompt: *"Design a Postgres schema for [your app]"*
options.alternative: ChatGPT

options.startHere: **Claude** — prompt: *"Write user stories for [feature] from a beginner user's perspective"*
options.alternative: Notion AI 💳
Obsidian 🟢
FigJam 🟢

title: Design Inspiration
description: Before you design anything, look at what already works. Don't start from a blank canvas. Steal like an artist. Copy the structure, never the content.

options.alternative: Lapa Ninja 🟢
Refero 🟢
Godly 🟢
Layers.to 🟢
Behance 🟢

title: UI Design Tool
description: Where you actually design what your app looks like before (or instead of) coding it. If you're not a designer, skip straight to building with AI tools in step 06.

options.alternative: Use if…
You want industry-standard design, collaboration
You want Figma but self-hosted, no cost
You want to design AND publish without coding
You just need to sketch a rough wireframe quickly

title: UI Components
description: Pre-built buttons, cards, menus, modals — everything visual in your app. Don't build these yourself. Copy-paste from these libraries.

options.alternative: Best for
The default choice. Dark mode, Tailwind, copy-paste. Used by everyone.
Landing page "wow" effects — animated borders, glowing cards, typing text
Premium motion components — parallax, 3D cards, spotlight hover
Marketplace of shadcn-style hero sections and bento grids
If you need a full component system with charts, dates, complex inputs
Simpler apps, less customisation needed, quickest to start

title: Vibe Coding / AI Builders
description: Tools that write the code FOR you. Describe what you want, get working code. This is where 80% of beginners should actually start — before writing a single line manually. *"Build a [type of app] with [Next.js / React]. It should have [feature 1], [feature 2]. Use Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components. Dark mode by default."*

options.alternative: Use if…
You want a single component or page, copy the code into your project
You want a full app scaffolded and running in the browser instantly
You want a full-stack app (React + Supabase) from a prompt, hosted
You have an existing project, want AI to work inside your real codebase
VSCode with AI built in — best for developers who know some code
You want to code AND deploy in the browser, no local setup
Autonomous AI agent that builds, runs, and deploys for you asynchronously

title: Frontend Framework
description: The foundation your app is built on. Think of this as choosing the type of house before decorating. If you don't know which to pick — pick Next.js. Decision made.

options.alternative: Use if…
Building ANY web app. The safe, powerful default.
You want a simple SPA, no server-side rendering needed
Your site is mostly content (blog, docs, marketing) — fastest option
You want something lighter than React with better performance
You prefer Vue over React

title: Backend / BaaS
description: The "server" part that stores data, handles logins, runs logic. BaaS (Backend as a Service) = someone else runs the server for you.

options.alternative: Use if…
You need a database + auth + storage + API all in one. Best choice for 90% of apps.
You know Google tools, need realtime data (chat, live updates)
You want Supabase but fully open-source and self-hosted
Tiny single-file backend for small projects. Download and run.
Realtime sync between server and client without WebSocket code

title: Database
description: Where your app's data actually lives — users, posts, products, anything that needs to be saved.

options.alternative: Use if…
You're already using Supabase. Just use this.
Serverless Postgres, great for Next.js apps, auto-scales to zero
MySQL, branches like Git for your database schema
SQLite at the edge — fast, cheap, good for global apps
You prefer document storage over tables (JSON-like data)

title: Authentication
description: Login, signup, "forgot password", Google/GitHub login buttons. DO NOT build this yourself. Use a library or service.

options.alternative: Use if…
TypeScript app, you want full control, no per-user billing
You're already on Supabase — auth is built in, just enable it
You want beautiful pre-built login UI in minutes, don't mind paying at scale
Next.js app, want open-source, comfortable with some config
Enterprise features, social logins, fine-grained permissions

title: Storage
description: Where uploaded files live — profile pictures, documents, videos, anything users upload.

options.alternative: Use if…
Already on Supabase — included, S3-compatible, easy
Zero egress fees — you don't pay to READ files back (unlike S3)
You need enterprise scale, fine-grained access control
Dead-simple file uploads in Next.js — npm install and done
You're on Firebase already

title: Payments
description: Charging users money. Subscriptions, one-time purchases, free trials.

options.alternative: Use if…
You want maximum control, subscriptions, invoices, the industry standard
They handle VAT/sales tax globally for you — huge for solo founders
Similar to Lemon Squeezy, better for enterprise pricing
Best choice if you're charging Indian users

title: Email
description: Sending emails to your users — welcome emails, password resets, notifications, newsletters.

options.alternative: Use if…
Write emails as React components. Best DX available.
Resend + email marketing combined — one tool for transactional AND campaigns
Fast, reliable, great deliverability, no free tier
Enterprise, more complex, overkill for most
For newsletters and marketing campaigns, not transactional
Building a newsletter with growth features built in

title: Notifications
description: Push notifications (mobile), in-app notifications (bell icon), SMS alerts.

options.alternative: Use if…
One API for email + in-app + push + SMS + Slack — open source
Push notifications for web and mobile apps
Enterprise notification infrastructure with workflows

title: Analytics
description: Understanding who visits your site, what they click, where they drop off. Install this DAY ONE. You cannot improve what you don't measure.

options.alternative: Use if…
Replaces Google Analytics + Mixpanel + Hotjar. Open source. One install.
Privacy-first, GDPR compliant, simple dashboard, no cookies
Plausible alternative, host it yourself on Railway for free
Free but complex, data goes to Google
Product analytics, funnels, cohorts for apps

title: Deployment
description: Making your app accessible on the internet.

options.alternative: Use if…
Next.js app (made by same team). Push to GitHub = deployed.
Static sites, React SPAs, good alternative to Vercel
Ultra fast CDN, global edge, very generous free tier
Use if…
Detects your framework, deploys, attaches database — easiest backend deploy
Like Railway, free tier available but spins down after inactivity
Global deployment, fast, Docker-based, more config needed
Self-hosted Heroku on your own VPS. Cheap at scale.

title: Domain
description: Your `.com` address. Costs ~$10–15/year.

options.alternative: Why
Cheapest prices, no markup, best DNS management included
Clean UI, easy to use, reliable
Often cheapest for first year deals

title: Security
description: Protecting your app from bots, spam, attacks, and abuse.

options.alternative: Use if…
DDoS protection, firewall, bot blocking — put it in front of everything
Rate limiting, bot detection, email validation in your Next.js API — npm install
Cloudflare's CAPTCHA alternative — user-friendly, no puzzle
Google's CAPTCHA — familiar but more intrusive UX

title: SEO
description: Getting your site to show up on Google when people search for your product.

options.alternative: Use if…
See what queries bring people to your site. Non-negotiable.
Free version shows broken links, keyword data for your own site
AI-driven content optimization if you're writing blog content

title: Monitoring & Errors
description: Getting notified when something breaks BEFORE your users tell you.

options.alternative: Use if…
Error tracking — you see full stack traces when something crashes
Uptime monitoring — alerts you when your site goes down
Error tracking + session replay combined, open source

title: AI APIs
description: Connecting your app to AI models — for chatbots, text generation, summaries, anything AI-powered.

options.alternative: Use if…
One API key, 100+ models. **Start here.** Swap models without changing code.
Fastest inference available. Free. LLaMA 3.3 70B at 320 tokens/sec.
Best for coding, analysis, long documents, nuanced reasoning
GPT-4o, best multimodal (images + text), huge ecosystem
Gemini free tier, 1M token context — best free option for large documents
Even faster than Groq, less known, great backup

title: Search
description: Letting users search your content, tools, products, posts — anything in your database.

options.alternative: Use if…
Fast, typo-tolerant, beautiful filters. Open source.
Same as Meilisearch, slightly easier to run
Managed (they host it), most polished DX, expensive at scale
Client-side fuzzy search for small datasets (under 10k items) — no server needed

title: Automation
description: Making things happen automatically without you doing them manually. "When someone signs up, send them a welcome email and add them to Notion."

options.alternative: Use if…
Open-source Zapier. Self-host on Railway free tier. 400+ integrations.
Easiest to use, most integrations, gets expensive fast
Visual automation, cheaper than Zapier, more complex than Zapier

title: Launch
description: Telling the world your product exists.

options.alternative: Best for
Traffic spike on launch day, exposure to early adopters
Developer tools, technical products — can go viral
r/SideProject, r/webdev, r/entrepreneur — real feedback
Build-in-public community, best for ongoing updates
Founder community, milestone posts, genuine engagement
Write about HOW you built it — drives developer traffic

title: Full Starter Stacks
description: Copy-paste one of these. Don't mix and match until you understand each piece.

options.alternative: Monthly
~$1 (amortised)
